seoTitle: Hutton Creek vs Iama Island — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Hutton Creek and Iama Island. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Hutton Creek vs Iama Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hutton Creek and Iama Island.
heading: Hutton Creek vs Iama Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hutton Creek, Queensland and Iama Island, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Iama Island has a lower overall climate risk score (27/100) compared to Hutton Creek (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hutton Creek or Iama Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hutton Creek has a flood risk score of 38/100, while Iama Island scores 35/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hutton Creek scores 57/100 for bushfire risk. Iama Island scores 45/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hutton Creek or Iama Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Iama Island has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.8% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
